title: ComfyUI Tutorial Series Ep 62: Nunchaku Update | Qwen Control Net, Qwen Edit & Inpaint
description:
In this episode, we set up a clean, portable ComfyUI with the latest Nunchaku nodes, then build and test practical workflows for Qwen ControlNet, Qwen Edit, and inpainting. You’ll see a fresh install from scratch, how to map your existing model folders, and how to run it. Testing Flux workflows (text-to-image, image-to-image, LoRA stack), and finish with real-world ControlNet use cases (canny, depth, pose) plus Qwen Edit single-image, two-image stitch, and masked inpaint.

What you’ll learn:

- Reliable fresh install of a portable ComfyUI + Nunchaku (no leftover conflicts)
- Linking existing model paths to avoid duplicates
- Installing Nunchaku wheels & optional Sage Attention for speed
- Flux T2I/I2I with upscaling, tiling tips, VRAM presets
- Qwen for Nunchaku: model choices (INT4/FP4, 32 vs 128, lightning), seeds, sizing, quality tweaks
- Qwen ControlNet: canny, depth, pose
- Qwen ControlNet Inpaint
- Qwen Edit: instruction-style prompts (replace/add/change), two-image edits, and in
